SAN FRANCISCO вҖ” January 15, 2025 вҖ” Lumina today announced the launch of its flagship product: a smart desk lamp that automatically adjusts color temperature and brightness based on time of day, calendar events, and ambient room lighting. Priced at $149, Lumina is now available for purchase at getlumina.com with free shipping for launch week orders.

Unlike traditional desk lamps that emit a fixed light output regardless of conditions, Lumina uses a trio of integrated sensors and cloud calendar integration to deliver context-aware lighting throughout the workday вҖ” without requiring manual adjustments or a companion app.

"Remote workers have optimized everything on their desks except the one thing that affects their eyes, energy, and sleep the most: their light," said [FOUNDER NAME], founder and CEO of Lumina. "We built Lumina to be the lamp you never have to think about. It reads your room, reads your schedule, and just does the right thing."

THE PROBLEM: STATIC LIGHT IN A DYNAMIC WORKDAY

The shift to remote work has placed millions of professionals in home environments with inconsistent and often inadequate lighting. Research from the American Optometric Association indicates that properly calibrated workspace lighting can reduce reported eye strain by up to 51%, yet most desk lamps offer no adaptive capabilities whatsoever.

Remote workers routinely transition between video calls, heads-down focus sessions, and evening work вҖ” each demanding different lighting conditions. Traditional lamps force users to either manually adjust settings throughout the day or accept a one-size-fits-all light that serves no single task well.

HOW LUMINA WORKS

Lumina addresses this through three core capabilities:

1. TIME-OF-DAY ADAPTATION: Lumina automatically shifts color temperature throughout the day вҖ” cool, energizing light (5000K) in the morning, balanced neutral light (4000K) at midday, and warm tones (2700K) in the evening вҖ” supporting the body's natural circadian rhythm.

2. CALENDAR SYNC: By connecting to Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook, Lumina detects upcoming video calls and adjusts brightness and color temperature for optimal on-camera appearance. During focus blocks, it shifts to softer, non-distracting light.

3. AMBIENT LIGHT SENSING: A built-in sensor continuously measures room light levels and compensates in real time вҖ” brightening on overcast mornings and dialing back when afternoon sun floods the workspace.

Setup takes under two minutes. The lamp connects via WiFi and requires no hub, no proprietary app, and no subscription. An optional web dashboard allows users to customize preferences, but Lumina is designed to work autonomously out of the box.

PRICING AND AVAILABILITY

Lumina is available starting today at getlumina.com in two colorways: Matte Black and Warm White.

вҖў Retail price: $149 USD
вҖў Free shipping on all launch week orders (through January 22, 2025)
вҖў 30-day risk-free return policy
вҖў 50,000-hour LED lifespan (17+ years at typical use)

COMPETITIVE POSITIONING

Lumina enters the market between budget desk lamps that lack smart features and premium options priced at $500+. The BenQ ScreenBar ($109) offers no adaptive color temperature or calendar integration. The Dyson Lightcycle ($599) provides time-of-day adaptation but lacks calendar sync and requires a dedicated app. Lumina delivers the most comprehensive feature set in its category at a price point accessible to the average remote worker.

High-resolution product images, lifestyle photography, founder headshots, and a product demo video are available in the Lumina press kit at getlumina.com/press.

SAN FRANCISCO вҖ” January 15, 2025 вҖ” Lumina today announced the launch of its flagship product: a smart desk lamp that automatically adjusts color temperature and brightness based on time of day, calendar events, and ambient room lighting. Priced at $149, Lumina is now available for purchase at getlumina.com with free shipping for launch week orders.

Unlike traditional desk lamps that emit a fixed light output regardless of conditions, Lumina uses a trio of integrated sensors and cloud calendar integration to deliver context-aware lighting throughout the workday вҖ” without requiring manual adjustments or a companion app.

"Remote workers have optimized everything on their desks except the one thing that affects their eyes, energy, and sleep the most: their light," said [FOUNDER NAME], founder and CEO of Lumina. "We built Lumina to be the lamp you never have to think about. It reads your room, reads your schedule, and just does the right thing."

THE PROBLEM: STATIC LIGHT IN A DYNAMIC WORKDAY

The shift to remote work has placed millions of professionals in home environments with inconsistent and often inadequate lighting. Research from the American Optometric Association indicates that properly calibrated workspace lighting can reduce reported eye strain by up to 51%, yet most desk lamps offer no adaptive capabilities whatsoever.

Remote workers routinely transition between video calls, heads-down focus sessions, and evening work вҖ” each demanding different lighting conditions. Traditional lamps force users to either manually adjust settings throughout the day or accept a one-size-fits-all light that serves no single task well.

HOW LUMINA WORKS

Lumina addresses this through three core capabilities:

1. TIME-OF-DAY ADAPTATION: Lumina automatically shifts color temperature throughout the day вҖ” cool, energizing light (5000K) in the morning, balanced neutral light (4000K) at midday, and warm tones (2700K) in the evening вҖ” supporting the body's natural circadian rhythm.

2. CALENDAR SYNC: By connecting to Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook, Lumina detects upcoming video calls and adjusts brightness and color temperature for optimal on-camera appearance. During focus blocks, it shifts to softer, non-distracting light.

3. AMBIENT LIGHT SENSING: A built-in sensor continuously measures room light levels and compensates in real time вҖ” brightening on overcast mornings and dialing back when afternoon sun floods the workspace.

Setup takes under two minutes. The lamp connects via WiFi and requires no hub, no proprietary app, and no subscription. An optional web dashboard allows users to customize preferences, but Lumina is designed to work autonomously out of the box.

PRICING AND AVAILABILITY

Lumina is available starting today at getlumina.com in two colorways: Matte Black and Warm White.

COMPETITIVE POSITIONING

Lumina enters the market between budget desk lamps that lack smart features and premium options priced at $500+. The BenQ ScreenBar ($109) offers no adaptive color temperature or calendar integration. The Dyson Lightcycle ($599) provides time-of-day adaptation but lacks calendar sync and requires a dedicated app. Lumina delivers the most comprehensive feature set in its category at a price point accessible to the average remote worker.
